description: You are a web developer at a mid-size e-commerce company expanding into Japan, Germany, and Brazil. A junior developer attempted to add i18n support to the Node.js storefront, but the implementation has critical bugs: Japanese customers see JPY prices with decimal places (¥1,234.56), German product pages show grammatically broken plurals ('1 Artikels'), dates appear in US MM/DD/YYYY format for all locales, template variables like '{customerName}' render as literal text, and missing translations cause infinite fallback loops. Fix these 5 bugs in the codebase at /home/ga/workspace/shopfront-i18n/ so that 'npm test' passes all 15 test assertions.
